From fused sensor data to verifiable biomass, absorption forecasts, and registry-ready documentation.

One platform. Four carbon-critical layers.

We convert heterogeneous remote-sensing data into high-fidelity forest intelligence for credible climate accounting.

Input

LiDAR, UAV, SAR, optical

Airborne and drone LiDAR are fused with Sentinel-1 backscatter and Sentinel-2 multispectral data for structural and spectral context.

AI core

Vision LLM crown delineation

A domain-tuned model separates individual tree crowns and canopy layers in dense, overlapping coniferous stands.

Output

Carbon stock and absorption

3D canopy models are translated into biomass, carbon stock, annual uptake, change alerts, and forecast scenarios.

Technology shown as measurable forest structure.

Airborne LiDAR RGB point clouds turn forests into spatial evidence: visible canopy height, crown texture, stand density, and terrain context become the input layer for AI carbon intelligence.

CaptureAirborne and UAV LiDAR record the forest as dense 3D points, preserving height and crown structure beyond 2D imagery.
InterpretA vision LLM separates individual crowns and canopy layers from overlapping coniferous stands.
ReportGeometry becomes biomass, carbon stock, annual absorption, and audit-ready MRV documentation.

From ground reference to AI-readable carbon structure.

Ground LiDAR supplies the close-range evidence needed to calibrate airborne models. The raw point cloud records tree geometry at stem, branch, and crown level; cluster classification converts that complexity into separated objects for training, validation, and carbon estimation.

ReferenceTLS and mobile LiDAR establish high-resolution ground truth for individual trees and canopy components.
ClusterAI segmentation separates crowns, stems, and overlapping structures into measurable object groups.
CalibrateGround clusters validate airborne crown delineation and reduce uncertainty in biomass estimation.
TranslateSegmented geometry becomes carbon stock, annual absorption, and audit-ready MRV evidence.

The problem is not data. It is trust.

Forest carbon markets require evidence that is current, precise, repeatable, and affordable. Traditional inventories are too slow; generic satellite products are too uncertain.

Manual inventories lag reality

Decade-scale sampling cannot support dynamic carbon reporting or fast disturbance detection.

2D imagery misses canopy structure

Carbon accounting needs vertical architecture, crown geometry, and stand density - not only surface reflectance.

Verification costs exclude smaller owners

Lower MRV costs can open carbon finance to small and medium forest enterprises.

Built for the European nature-based carbon economy.

Initial focus: coniferous forests in northern Germany. Expansion path: broader northern Europe and crop-specific biomass services.

Forest ownersAnnual carbon-stock reports, change detection, and asset-level climate value.
State agenciesRegion-wide monitoring, policy evidence, and repeatable public reporting.
Carbon buyersVerified nature-based offset documentation for net-zero commitments.
Agri-carbonFuture retraining for crop biomass and soil-carbon advisory services.

Differentiation by precision, fusion, and compliance.

The venture sits between broad remote-sensing platforms and slow consultancies: automated enough to scale, specialized enough to verify.

Northern European canopy model

Trained for coniferous morphology rather than generic global forest assumptions.

Native multi-sensor fusion

Combines LiDAR structure with SAR and optical signals to reduce dependence on costly repeated flights.

Registry-ready MRV

Designed around European carbon-reporting requirements and carbon-credit dossier generation.

Already beyond concept.

The team brings validated point-cloud workflows, field acquisition capability, forestry domain knowledge, AI engineering, finance, and GIS visualization.

Processed forest LiDAR datasets

Prior work includes airborne, drone, terrestrial, and handheld mobile LiDAR data across coniferous forest contexts.

Segmentation evidence

Tree canopy and ground-reference segmentation experiments demonstrate a practical technical starting point.

Operational assets

Partner access supports drone platforms, forest survey resources, calibration data, and recurring validation materials.

Roadmap to audit-ready deployment.

From test site validation near Bodensee to scalable SaaS reporting for forest carbon portfolios.

01 · Test site

Establish pilot datasets and validation protocol in Baden-Württemberg near Bodensee.

02 · Model tuning

Train and benchmark canopy delineation on GPU infrastructure and EuroHPC resources.

03 · Carbon ledger

Generate biomass, stock, absorption, forecast, and dossier workflows.

04 · Market entry

Launch tiered SaaS for owners, agencies, verifiers, and enterprise offset buyers.

Turn forest structure into climate-grade evidence.

For pilot partners, forestry agencies, and carbon-credit stakeholders seeking precise, repeatable, and transparent MRV.
